# VER-QUIC-CRT-0006 - QUIC Transport Bridge, Diagnostics, and Sender/Recovery Verification

## Scope

This slice verifies the library-owned transport/runtime seams that future hosts and the interop harness will reuse. It does not prove end-to-end QUIC transport behavior.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0103
- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0104
- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0105

## Verification Method

Focused requirement-home tests and inspection evidence for the new library seam classes.

## Preconditions

- The Incursa.Quic solution builds successfully.
- The tests project can access internal library seams through the existing friend assembly relationship.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the new CRT and RFC9001 requirement-home tests covering the TLS bridge state, connection-runtime integration, sender cleanup, and the diagnostics sink.
- Inspect the seam implementation files to confirm they remain library-owned and do not embed interop-runner-specific behavior.
- Confirm the solution build and test pass include the new seam files.

## Expected Result

The TLS bridge state can represent transport-parameter, key-lifecycle, handshake-confirmation, and fatal-alert facts; the connection reducer consumes those bridge outputs; Initial and Handshake key discards clear retained sender state and PTO backoff; the diagnostics sink is cheap when disabled; and the sender/recovery owner retains packet-sent state and PTO ownership in the library.
